  "findings": [
    "Staged conductor is undersized for the combined load and circuit length, resulting in excessive voltage drop",
    "Conductor ampacity and voltage drop not verified against NEC Table 310.16 and circuit length"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Recalculate required conductor size using load current and one-way circuit length",
    "Upsize conductor to keep voltage drop within 3% for feeders or 5% total (feeder + branch circuit) per NEC recommended practice",
    "Verify final selection against Table 310.16 ampacity and 75°C column unless equipment rating dictates otherwise",
    "Replace staged undersized conductor before any installation begins"
  ],
  "rationale": "The staged conductor is non-compliant because it is undersized for the load and circuit length, which will cause excessive voltage drop, overheating risk, and equipment malfunction. NEC 310 and Table 310.16 govern conductor ampacity; while voltage drop recommendations are found in NEC informational notes, a conductor that cannot carry the load safely violates 310.16 ampacity requirements. The correct approach is to upsize the conductor to satisfy both ampacity and voltage drop limits before installation.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 310",
    "Table 310.16"
  ]
}
